# Hand-Corrected Generated Classes

This folder holds files that **originated as xsd2code++ output** but have since been
**hand-corrected** to fix defects in the generated code. They are no longer pristine
generated artifacts, so they must **not** live in `SDC Unmodified Classes/` (the pristine
generated set). Each file here carries a header block documenting its origin, the
correction, and how to re-apply the correction after a future regeneration.

## Why these files are NOT auto-generated-in-place

Editing files under `SDC Unmodified Classes/` directly would (a) violate the
"don't edit `<auto-generated>` files in place" policy and (b) be silently overwritten by
the next xsd2code++ run. Moving the corrected copy here (via `git mv`, preserving history)
makes the alteration explicit and reviewable.

## Files

| File | Issue | Correction |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `DataTypes_DEType.cs` | #10 | Fixed the `DataTypes_DEType.Item` polymorphic choice `[XmlElement]` bindings: `gYearMonth` was bound to `gMonth_DEtype` (wrong CLR type) → corrected to `gYearMonth_DEtype`; the missing `dayTimeDuration` element binding was added (`dayTimeDuration_DEtype`). Without this, `gMonth`/`gYearMonth`/`dayTimeDuration` response nodes could not be attached via the standard tree builder (by-CLR-type element-name resolution found 2 matches for `gMonth_DEtype` and 0 for the other two). |

## Regen survival (READ BEFORE RE-RUNNING xsd2code++)

A future xsd2code++ regeneration will emit a **fresh, uncorrected** copy of each of these
files back into its original generated folder (e.g. `SDC Unmodified Classes/DataTypes_DEType.cs`).

Because both folders are compiled and these are `partial class` definitions that re-declare
the same members, the regenerated copy will **collide with the corrected copy here and fail
the build** — this is a deliberate, loud safety net (far better than a silently reverted fix).

After any regen you MUST:
1. **Delete** the freshly regenerated copy from its original generated folder
   (e.g. `SDC Unmodified Classes/DataTypes_DEType.cs`).
2. **Re-apply** the corrections — search the new output for the markers
   `ISSUE #10 HAND-CORRECTION` in the corresponding file here and port the same edits — then
   keep the corrected file in this folder.
3. Rebuild and run the date round-trip tests
   (`DateResponseTypeRoundTripTests`, `DateResponseTypeBoundaryTests`) to confirm the
   `gMonth`/`gYearMonth`/`dayTimeDuration` standard-API construction still works.

If/when the xsd2code++ template itself is fixed to emit the correct bindings, these files can
be retired back into the pristine generated set and this folder removed.
